behavior_summary: Implements the NPC Torwin's interactive and autonomous behaviors: descriptive 'U8P_TORWIN::look' output, schedule-based autonomous actions, proximity (fast-area) entry/exit handling, theft reaction, and responses to spell casts.
TORWIN::look composes and emits a short identification string when a player inspects Torwin; if Torwin is dead it uses a corpse-style label.
A schedule system (U8P_TORWIN::schedule and spawned helpers) selects, records (torwinSchedule), and spawns behavior routines to drive periodic movement, approach, or talk actions.
Fast-area entry (U8P_TORWIN::enterFastArea) either teleports Torwin to random coordinates or dispatches schedule-specific behaviors depending on torwinSchedule, and clears per-slot refs.

AvatarStoleSomething detects theft, resets related references, and — unless a particular schedule is active — makes Torwin approach the thief, bark an admonishment, set activity/animation, and trigger timers/effects.

cast handles spells targeting Torwin or nearby actors; it contains logic that can remove immortal status and set Torwin dead, spawn timed effects, play animations/sounds, and teleport Torwin away for dramatic death reactions.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: individual named handlers run in response to game events (U8P_TORWIN::look, enterFastArea, leaveFastArea, cast, AvatarStoleSomething, and spawned schedule helpers).
Look handler short-circuits if Npc::isDead -> corpse label; otherwise uses two global flags (torwinName, torwinJob) to choose a string variant and calls Item::bark.
Schedule selection routine (U8P_TORWIN::schedule) queries FREE::* checks and torwinGottaJump to pick a schedule id, spawns METHOD::143A to start it, and writes torwinSchedule on success.
enterFastArea uses urandom to generate coordinates and may teleport Torwin randomly, or branch into schedule-specific spawn logic based on torwinSchedule values (0,1,2...).

AvatarStoleSomething loops through stored refs/slots to find a matching thief/owner; on match it computes direction (Item::getDirToItem) and spawns a movement METHOD to approach, guarded by a torwinSchedule==1 early exit.
Spawned helpers (U8P_TORWIN::0531, U8P_TORWIN::0623) perform probabilistic checks and pathfinding, and are guarded by somebodyTalking to avoid interrupting other dialog.
open_questions: Exact textual variants used by TORWIN::look (full strings and their localization) are inferred but not exhaustively listed here.
Precise semantics and numeric meanings of schedule ids stored in torwinSchedule (what each id maps to in game behavior) are only inferred from branching and spawns.

Exact meanings and side-effects of the various FREE::* calls (e.g., FREE::2A56, FREE::28F9, FREE::1C19) are library-level and partially inferred (timers/effects/ref clearing); their full behavior is unknown.
Contents and structure of the reference slots scanned by AvatarStoleSomething and helpers (what each slot represents) are inferred but not concretely identified.
Whether teleport coordinates computed from urandom are absolute map coords or offsets/tiles requires engine context not present in the disassembly.

main_function.name: U8P_TORWIN::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player 'U8P_TORWIN::look' (examine) action on Torwin. If Torwin is dead, emit a corpse-style label; otherwise, compose a concise identification string based on global flags (torwinName, torwinJob) and make Torwin 'bark' it to the viewer.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the 'U8P_TORWIN::look' event when a player or actor inspects the Torwin NPC.

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::look
function_map.role: Handle player's 'U8P_TORWIN::look' inspect event for Torwin, produce appropriate identification string or corpse label and call Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::schedule
function_map.role: Choose and start an autonomous behavior schedule for Torwin, write torwinSchedule, and spawn schedule execution methods (METHOD::143A / METHOD::133F).

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry handler: teleport Torwin randomly or dispatch schedule-specific spawned behaviors; clear/reset per-slot refs via FREE::2A56.

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit handler: clear/reset references and per-area state to clean up when avatar leaves proximity.

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: React to theft when Torwin is aware: reset refs, find thief by scanning slots, approach thief (spawn movement method), bark admonishment, set activity/timers; gated by torwinSchedule.

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::cast
function_map.role: Handle spell-cast events involving Torwin or nearby targets; can perform non-lethal reactions or trigger a scripted death (clrImmortal, setDead, spawn effects, teleport).

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::0531
function_map.role: Spawned schedule behavior: iterate reference slots, attempt pathfind/approach, and perform speech/animation if target found and nobody else is talking.

function_map.name: U8P_TORWIN::0623
function_map.role: Spawned helper used by schedules to perform conditional/randomized approaches and speech/timers; supports probabilistic/reactive behavior.

internal_calls.purpose: May spawn or route to schedule-specific spawned behaviors (one of multiple schedule execution paths).
enterFastArea uses schedule state and may invoke schedule-related routines to execute schedule-specific logic rather than teleporting.
After initiating theft reaction (speech/animation), flow re-enters/uses enterFastArea logic to proceed with proximity-driven behavior.
Spawned behavior may call helper 0623 to perform probabilistic checks, approach decisions, or follow-up speech/timer sequences.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether Torwin is dead to choose corpse label vs alive identification behavior.
Display/speak the composed identification string to the viewer/player.
Generate random X/Y values used for teleport coordinates when performing random teleport behavior.
Teleport Torwin to computed/random coordinates on area entry or after certain cast/death reactions.
Query internal/free-data flags or state to decide schedule parameters (used when choosing schedule behavior).
Clear or check reference slots/state associated with area triggers; used for cleanup and gating behaviors.
Clear references for stolen items/slots before processing theft reaction.
Compute direction from Torwin to the stolen item/thief to spawn an approach movement METHOD.
Spawn movement/approach behavior toward the thief/item (engine navigation method).
Clear immortality flag prior to setting Torwin dead in scripted death reaction.
Set Torwin's dead state as part of a scripted spell-related death reaction.
Spawn a timed effect or timer after a death reaction or other cast-related actions.
Attempt pathfinding toward a selected coordinate/target before performing speech/animation sequences.

reads.name: torwinName
reads.likely_effect: Selects which name fragment Torwin uses in the U8P_TORWIN::look/identify output (influences long vs short form).

reads.name: torwinJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether Torwin's job/title (e.g., 'monk') is included in the U8P_TORWIN::look identification string.

reads.name: torwinGottaJump
reads.likely_effect: When set, causes U8P_TORWIN::schedule to pick an alternate schedule (inferred 'jump' or special action).

reads.name: torwinSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Read as a gate to decide which schedule-specific behavior to execute on area entry or theft; also used to early-exit theft response when equal to specific values.

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Guard to avoid starting Torwin speech/interaction when another entity is currently talking.

writes.name: torwinSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_TORWIN::schedule to record the chosen behavior schedule id so subsequent handlers use that schedule's behavior.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the NPC Torwin's interactive and autonomous behaviors: descriptive 'U8P_TORWIN::look' output, schedule-based autonomous actions, proximity (fast-area) entry/exit handling, theft reaction, and responses to spell casts.
